review_gate_rows.required_reading: different false-positive or false-negative profiles across candidate buckets
review_gate_rows.why_it_matters: A label split is not justified if all candidate groups fail in the same way.

review_gate_rows.required_reading: freshness, persistence, or breadth-confirmation patterns differ materially across candidate buckets
review_gate_rows.why_it_matters: The split must reflect state meaning, not only arbitrary numerical separation.

review_gate_rows.required_reading: review complexity falls while sample buckets remain usable for bounded follow-up
review_gate_rows.why_it_matters: The split should clarify the stage, not shatter it into tiny unstable shards.

rejection_gate_rows.reading: Keep refinement on the feature side only if candidate buckets do not carry different semantic-v2 patterns.
Reject label-split candidacy if the buckets are merely symbol-specific rather than stage-specific.
Reject label-split candidacy if candidate buckets become too small to support bounded follow-up review.

review_questions: Does high_level_consolidation contain at least one candidate substate with clearly different semantic-v2 meaning?
Would a bounded split improve decision quality more than further feature refinement alone?
Can the candidate split remain review-only first, without pretending to legislate a new formal label schema?
interpretation: This protocol does not authorize a label split by itself.
It defines the review threshold that candidate buckets must clear before label refinement becomes a lawful bounded follow-up.
The current default remains feature-side refinement unless candidate structure shows distinct semantics plus distinct error behavior.
